sentence: This old phone is completely _____ now. Nobody uses it.
explanation: USE → useless (add -less to mean 'without use')

sentence: The task was _____ . We finished it in five minutes.
explanation: DO → doable (add -able to mean 'can be done')

sentence: She found the answer _____ . It was not what she expected.
explanation: BELIEVE → unbelievable (un- + believe + -able = not able to be believed)

sentence: The _____ of the old building was completed last month.
explanation: CONSTRUCT → reconstruction (re- + construction = building again)

sentence: His behaviour was completely _____ at the dinner party.
explanation: ACCEPT → unacceptable (un- + accept + -able = not acceptable)

sentence: The _____ of the team led to their defeat.
explanation: ABLE → inability (in- + abil + -ity = lack of ability)

sentence: She showed great _____ during the crisis.
explanation: RELY → reliability (rely + -abil + -ity = quality of being reliable)

sentence: The instructions were _____ . Nobody could follow them.
explanation: READ → unreadable (un- + read + -able = not able to be read)

sentence: The _____ of the contract caused many problems.
explanation: RENEW → renewal (re- + new + -al = the act of renewing)

sentence: Her _____ to the team was invaluable.
explanation: CONTRIBUTE → contribution (change -e to -ion to make a noun)

sentence: The food was _____ . We couldn't eat it.
explanation: EAT → uneatable (un- + eat + -able = not fit to be eaten)

sentence: The _____ of the old factory created new jobs.
explanation: DEVELOP → redevelopment (re- + develop + -ment = developing again)

sentence: His _____ to change was frustrating for everyone.
explanation: WILLING → unwillingness (un- + willing + -ness = not being willing)

sentence: The situation was _____ . There was nothing we could do.
explanation: AVOID → unavoidable (un- + avoid + -able = cannot be avoided)

sentence: The _____ of the old system was necessary.
explanation: PLACE → replacement (re- + place + -ment = putting something new in place)

sentence: She felt a sense of _____ after finishing the marathon.
explanation: ACHIEVE → achievement (add -ment to make a noun)

sentence: The _____ of the data was a serious problem.
explanation: SECURE → insecurity (in- + secur + -ity = lack of security)

sentence: The new policy was _____ to many employees.
explanation: FAIR → unfair (un- + fair = not fair)

sentence: The _____ of the project was ahead of schedule.
explanation: COMPLETE → completion (change -e to -ion to make a noun)

sentence: His _____ was clear from the way he spoke.
explanation: HAPPY → unhappiness (un- + happi + -ness = state of not being happy)

sentence: The bridge was _____ after the earthquake.
explanation: PASS → impassable (im- + pass + -able = cannot be passed)

sentence: The _____ of the old park brought the community together.
explanation: STORE → restoration (re- + stor + -ation = the act of restoring)

sentence: Her _____ to learn new things is impressive.
explanation: EAGER → eagerness (add -ness to make a noun)

sentence: The problem was _____ without more information.
explanation: SOLVE → unsolvable (un- + solv + -able = cannot be solved)

sentence: The _____ of the old documents took several weeks.
explanation: DISCOVER → rediscovery (re- + discover + -y = discovering again)
